page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4a server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This configuration is designed for compute-bound workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand large memory capacities.

The instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its high vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4a-highcpu-8 offers a resource-dense profile optimized for raw processing power over memory capacity. This makes it a cost-efficient choice for workloads where CPU utilization is high but memory footprint is low. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics, and CI/CD pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4a family, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. This resource allocation provides a compute-optimized ratio of 2.0 GB of memory per core. The server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. It also does not feature complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for compute-heavy tasks with modest memory requirements, this instance offers a resource-dense configuration that is qualitatively cost-efficient for processing-intensive workloads. It is suited for applications such as batch processing, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines.
